---
title: Some numerics (numpy)
format: 
    pdf: default
    ipynb: default
jupyter: python3
---

We will be using numpy {#sec-numpy}

## Linear algebra (np.array etc)

This package provides powerful and fast computations for numerical mathematics. (Most of it is implemented in `C`.) We will only learn about some parts of it, some linear algebra and some optimization. Usually, it is imported via
```{python}
import numpy as np
```
(Then, the functions from `numpy` are called `np.something`.) Together with `pandas` (see @sec-pandas) for data handling, this is the basic framework for data analysis in Python3. 

Let us start with linear algebra. A vector or matrix is an `np.array`. `numpy` interprets a vector as a column vector, and 

```{python}
x = np.array([1.0, 2.0, 3.0])
A = np.array([
    [1.0, 0.0, 2.0],
    [0.0, 1.0, 1.0]
])

print("x =")
print(x)
print()
print("A =")
print(A)
print()
print("x.shape =", x.shape)
print("A.shape =", A.shape)
print("x.ndim =", x.ndim)
print("A.ndim =", A.ndim)
print("x.dtype =", x.dtype)
```

Generally we 




## Random numbers (np.rand)

## optimization (np.optimization)
